informal: Prove that a group of order 200 has a normal Sylow 5-subgroup.
lean:
theorem Dummit_Foote_exercise_4_5_18 {G : Type*} [Fintype G] [Group G]
  (hG : card G = 200) :
  ∃ N : Sylow 5 G, N.Normal
reasoning: The informal statement says a group of order 200 has a normal Sylow 5-subgroup. The Lean signature assumes G is a finite group with `card G = 200` and concludes `∃ N : Sylow 5 G, N.Normal`, i.e. there exists a Sylow 5-subgroup that is normal. Since all Sylow 5-subgroups are conjugate, the existence of one normal Sylow 5-subgroup is equivalent to the Sylow 5-subgroup being normal (and unique). The hypothesis (`Fintype G`, `Group G`, order 200) and conclusion match the informal claim exactly.
